body.title: Local mini-LLM fallback when graph-tree confidence is low
body.result: local_preparse.LocalPreparser class + preparse_local() convenience + default_preparser singleton. qwen2.5:0.5b default model, 1s default timeout, IGOR_LOCAL_PREPARSE_* env knobs. Returns PARSED_INPUT CSB block matching existing preparse schema. Local-only enforced via AST-strip test. 16 tests. Commit 47c943d1.
body.description:
When graph-tree gist-pass returns low confidence (per T-gist-before-retrieve), today we fall through to cortex.search. Add a middle tier: a local small-model LLM (Ollama with qwen 0.5B-1B or similar) that handles cases trees don't recognize but don't require full cortex retrieval. Zero network cost, sub-second latency on local hardware. Note: Akien explicitly removed web-LLM preparse (gpt5mini) from akiendell/akiendelllinux — this fallback must be LOCAL only.

**Affected files:** new `wild_igor/igor/cognition/local_preparse.py` wrapping an Ollama client; wire-in at the confidence-gated fallback point from T-gist-before-retrieve; `tests/test_local_preparse.py` new.
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (the local-only constraint is load-bearing — document clearly); test-plan-or-why-not (covered); oop-first (LocalPreparser class); no-sqlite.
**Scope boundary:** IN: Ollama client with small-model default (qwen 0.5B or configured equivalent); timeout ≤1s; returns same preparse schema as graph-tree path (intent, salience, entities, memory-route) so downstream is consumer-agnostic; instrumentation for invocation count and latency. OUT: cloud LLM fallback (explicitly excluded — local-only); model selection/tuning (config-driven, not hardcoded); training/fine-tuning the local model.
**Test plan:** low-confidence gist → local preparse invoked; local preparse returns same schema as graph-tree path; timeout triggers fall-through to cortex.search; unavailable Ollama triggers fall-through cleanly (no bork); latency ≤500ms p50 on CPU hardware; tests MUST NOT invoke cloud LLMs.
